utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills: The verifier failed test_all_customers_covered_exactly_once and test_route_capacity_not_exceeded and test_route_map_contains_map_library_reference. The route_map.html uses a custom SVG rather than folium/leaflet, which caused the map library reference check to fail. Regarding customer coverage, examining the wi_skills routes: vehicle 0 covers nodes {3,12,2,10,1}, vehicle 1 covers {9,6,4}, vehicle 2 covers {15,5,13,8}, vehicle 3 covers {14,11,7} — this totals nodes 1-15 exactly. However, the verifier still flagged it as failing, possibly due to a technical issue with how node IDs are indexed (the skill uses 1-indexed vehicle IDs but 0-based node IDs). The capacity check failure is unclear since all loads appear ≤150.
utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produce the same optimized route solution with total_distance=89214 and identical route assignments. However, they differ in key areas: (1) wo_skills passes all 6 verifier checks while wi_skills passes only 3, failing on customer coverage detection, capacity verification, and map library reference (SVG-based map vs Leaflet); (2) wi_skills includes a metrics section with fleet utilization rate (99.17%) which wo_skills lacks; (3) wo_skills uses Leaflet for an interactive map with real geographic coordinates and popups, which is more functional than wi_skills' static SVG map. Overall, wo_skills is more effective in terms of verifier compliance and map quality, while wi_skills has an advantage in reporting fleet utilization metrics. The wo_skills agent independently solved the CVRP to the same optimality as the skill-assisted agent, suggesting the baseline agent was capable enough for this particular instance.

utility_details.judge_error: Both wi_skills and wo_skills fail verifier V2-3 (routes missing arrival_times/departure_times arrays at route level) and V2-6 (shift limit check). For wi_skills, V2-5 also fails despite the agent claiming zero violations, suggesting the verifier detected actual time window violations in the output data. For wo_skills, V2-4 (metrics.time_window_violations field missing) and V2-5 also fail. The wi_skills agent correctly invoked /vehicle-routing-solver but the output JSON schema does not match the expected array format for arrival_times/departure_times.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is more effective than wo_skills overall. wi_skills: (1) explicitly invoked the /vehicle-routing-solver skill with OR-Tools VRPTW CumulVar constraints, (2) reports time_window_violations=0 and shift_violations=0 in a structured metrics object, (3) includes an unserved_customers field, and (4) produces a more detailed and structured output with explicit service time modeling. wo_skills: (1) lacks the time_window_violations metrics field entirely (verifier V2-4 fails), (2) has confirmed time window violations (verifier V2-5 fails), (3) omits the unserved_customers field, and (4) relies on ad-hoc OR-Tools usage without the VRPTW skill's specialized formulation. Both share the structural flaw of not producing route-level arrival_times/departure_times arrays (V2-3 fails for both), but wi_skills produces a more complete, verifiable, and operationally useful schedule with zero claimed violations backed by a proper VRPTW solver.

utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills run produced a plan where all 10 jobs are assigned to a single South Warehouse vehicle, leaving all North Warehouse vehicles unused. The verifier failed test_routes_from_both_depots, indicating the multi-depot constraint was not properly enforced. The solution is technically feasible for pairing constraints but fails multi-depot separation entirely. wo_skills failed test_paired_jobs_same_vehicle, indicating at least some pickup-delivery pairs were violated (delivery before pickup or split across vehicles).
utility_details.utility_summary: Neither solution is fully correct, but they fail on different dimensions. wi_skills correctly handles pickup-delivery pairing (all 10 pairs co-assigned with correct ordering, verifier passed), capacity constraints, and provides an unserved_jobs field, but completely fails multi-depot separation by routing everything through one South Warehouse vehicle — the North Warehouse fleet is entirely unused. wo_skills correctly implements multi-depot fleet separation (routes from both depots, geographic clustering), provides visual depot differentiation on the map, and includes per-depot summaries, but fails pickup-delivery pairing constraints (verifier failed test_paired_jobs_same_vehicle). For the specific task described (multi-depot matched pickup-delivery), the pairing constraint is operationally critical (crew must assemble/disassemble at both sites), making wi_skills more operationally valid for the core constraint, though both solutions are incomplete. Overall, wi_skills is slightly more effective because it correctly enforces the primary constraint (pickup-delivery pairing) which is described as the hardest and most critical aspect of this problem, even though it collapses to single-depot routing.
